methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $318M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Svante, a Series E climate and energy technology company founded by Claude Letourneau, has raised $318M to date. The comparable universe in Climate & Energy includes 1 public company (LNZA) and 8 private peers spanning adjacent stages and valuations, including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape.

The valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, as a peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage comparables within the narrow Climate & Energy peer set. The stage-progression method applies a 1.4x step-up multiplier typical of Series E advancement to the $318M total raised proxy, yielding an implied valuation of $445M. This represents the median point in the triangulated range.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $378M (low) to $512M (high), centered on $445M. Key uncertainties include the limited depth of exact-stage comparable transactions in the frontier climate-tech sector, which constrains confidence in stage-transition multiples. The reliance on a single methodology rather than multiple triangulation points introduces model risk. Material variables affecting valuation within this range include capital deployment pace, technology validation milestones, and the trajectory of Climate & Energy sector funding conditions.
